senses.gloss: to eat together with another, share in another's meal

examples.text: وَأَنَا كَيْفَ أُؤَاكِلُ أَقْوَامًا يَصْنَعُونَ هٰذَا الصَّنِيعَ ثُمَّ يَحْتَجُّونَ لَهُ بِمِثْلِ هَذِهِ الْحُجَجِ؟
examples.english: How can I eat with folk who do such as they do, and, in support of it, argue with such arguments as these?”

senses.gloss: to give something to eat, to feed
to bring one in another's power

examples.text: فَقَالَ لَهُ صَاحِبُهُ: لَوْلَا أَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ آكَلُ مِنِّي لَأَدْخَلْتُ لَكَ هٰذَا الْكَلَامَ فِي بَابِ النَّصِيحَةِ.
examples.english: And his friend said to him: If I were not to know that you are more esurient than I am, I would have commenced my advice with this introductory word.

etymology.text: See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.
